records.label: Runtime component tests
records.writes: temporary files only where component tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A runtime seam or engine/gateway/connector contract regressed.
records.notes: Primary Python reference-backend correctness lane.

records.label: Surface tests
records.writes: temporary files only where surface tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A web, CLI, or API projection regressed.
records.notes: Surfaces must stay on the public side of the architecture.

records.label: Repo-level tests
records.writes: temporary files only where integration tests create temp stores
records.failure_means: A cross-component, operations, eval, architecture, or parity guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Includes architecture, integration, operations, eval, and parity validation.

records.label: Architecture-boundary checker
records.failure_means: A Python import crossed an accepted runtime/gateway/surface/connectors boundary.
records.notes: Narrow repo-local import guardrail, not a universal policy engine.

records.label: Public-alpha smoke checks
records.failure_means: The constrained public-alpha allowed/blocked posture regressed.
records.notes: Does not deploy or expose a service.

records.label: Public-alpha hosting pack summary check
records.failure_means: The hosting pack route-safety summary drifted from the route inventory.
records.notes: Documentation consistency check only.

records.label: Python-oracle golden fixture check
records.failure_means: Committed Python oracle golden outputs drifted from current reference behavior.
records.notes: Required for Python oracle and future Rust parity discipline.

records.label: Archive-resolution eval runner
records.writes: temporary local index unless explicit output path is provided
records.failure_means: The hard-query archive-resolution guardrail no longer executes or reports honestly.
records.notes: Not a ranking, fuzzy, vector, LLM, crawling, or production relevance benchmark.

records.label: Search usefulness audit runner
records.writes: temporary local index unless explicit output path is provided
records.failure_means: The broad usefulness audit no longer produces local Eureka observations and pending manual baselines.
records.notes: External baselines must remain pending unless a human records observations.

records.label: Public-alpha route inventory validation
records.failure_means: Public-alpha route inventory structure or local-path classifications regressed.
records.notes: Pairs with public-alpha smoke checks.

records.label: Source registry runtime tests
records.failure_means: Source Registry v0 loading, validation, or filtering regressed.
records.notes: Keeps governed source inventory records coherent.

records.label: Source coverage and capability model tests
records.writes: temporary files only for malformed-record checks
records.failure_means: Source capability flags, coverage-depth values, or placeholder-source honesty regressed.
records.notes: Added by Source Coverage and Capability Model v0; it does not add connectors, live probes, or crawling.

records.label: Real source coverage pack tests
records.writes: temporary local indexes and stores only
records.failure_means: Recorded Internet Archive-like fixtures, local bundle fixtures, index visibility, public projection, or bounded member readback regressed.
records.notes: Added by Real Source Coverage Pack v0; it uses committed fixtures only and performs no live API calls, scraping, crawling, broad source federation, or arbitrary local filesystem ingestion.

records.label: Old-platform source coverage expansion tests
records.failure_means: Expanded old-platform recorded fixtures, local bundle members, compatibility evidence, local-index visibility, or public projection regressed.
records.notes: Added by Old-Platform Source Coverage Expansion v0; it validates committed fixtures only and performs no live API calls, scraping, crawling, broad source federation, arbitrary local filesystem ingestion, or real binary handling.

records.label: Old-platform software planner tests
records.failure_means: Deterministic old-platform OS aliases, latest-compatible intent, driver/hardware intent, vague identity uncertainty, or member-discovery hints regressed.
records.notes: Added by Old-Platform Software Planner Pack v0; it improves interpretation only and adds no ranking, fuzzy/vector retrieval, LLM planning, live source behavior, or source connectors.

records.label: Member-level synthetic records tests
records.failure_means: Deterministic synthetic member target refs, parent lineage, local-index visibility, search projection, or exact member resolution regressed.
records.notes: Added by Member-Level Synthetic Records v0; it uses committed local bundle fixtures only and adds no broad extraction, arbitrary local filesystem ingestion, ranking, live source behavior, or source connectors.

records.label: Result lanes and user-cost tests
records.failure_means: Deterministic result lanes, user-cost reasons, member-vs-parent cost behavior, or public search projection regressed.
records.notes: Added by Result Lanes + User-Cost Ranking v0; it validates bounded explanation hints only and adds no production ranking, fuzzy/vector retrieval, LLM scoring, live source behavior, or source connectors.

records.label: Compatibility evidence pack tests
records.failure_means: Source-backed compatibility evidence extraction, unknown outcomes, index projection, or surface rendering regressed.
records.notes: Added by Compatibility Evidence Pack v0; it validates fixture-backed evidence only and adds no compatibility oracle, installer execution, live source behavior, scraping, fuzzy/vector retrieval, LLM behavior, Rust behavior, or source connectors.

records.label: Test/eval operating layer validation
records.failure_means: The test registry, command matrix, or AIDE queue stopped being machine-readable.
records.notes: Added by Comprehensive Test/Eval Operating Layer and Repo Audit v0.

records.label: Comprehensive audit pack validation
records.failure_means: The comprehensive audit pack lost required files, structured findings, or anti-fake-success guardrails.
records.notes: Validates the repo-native audit outputs from this milestone.

records.label: Search usefulness backlog triage validation
records.failure_means: The search usefulness triage pack lost required docs, selected wedges, selected next milestone, or anti-fake-success guardrails.
records.notes: Added by Search Usefulness Backlog Triage v0; validates governance/backlog artifacts only.

records.label: Search usefulness audit delta validation
records.failure_means: The search usefulness delta pack lost required files, selected wedges, current counts, baseline limitations, or anti-fake-baseline guardrails.
records.notes: Added by Search Usefulness Audit Delta v0; validates audit/reporting artifacts only and changes no retrieval behavior.

records.label: Search usefulness audit delta v1 validation
records.failure_means: The search usefulness delta v1 pack lost required files, current counts, archive-eval movement, baseline limitations, or anti-fake-baseline guardrails.
records.notes: Added by Search Usefulness Audit Delta v1; validates audit/reporting artifacts only and changes no retrieval behavior.

records.label: Hard eval satisfaction pack validation
records.writes: temporary files only where the archive eval runner builds a transient local index
records.failure_means: The hard eval satisfaction report, source-backed partial movement, article capability gap, or anti-fake-satisfied guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Added by Hard Eval Satisfaction Pack v0; validates eval evidence mapping without weakening hard evals or changing source behavior.

records.label: Old-platform result refinement validation
records.writes: temporary files only where the archive eval runner builds a transient local index
records.failure_means: The old-platform result refinement report, strict driver satisfaction, remaining partial explanations, or no-fake-baseline guardrails regressed.
records.notes: Added by Old-Platform Result Refinement Pack v0; validates deterministic result-shape, lane, and bad-result checks without weakening hard evals or adding retrieval behavior.

records.label: More source coverage expansion v1 validation
records.writes: temporary files only where the archive eval runner builds a transient local index
records.failure_means: The More Source Coverage Expansion v1 report, source-backed hard-eval satisfaction, article capability gap, or no-live/no-real-binary guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Added by More Source Coverage Expansion v1; validates targeted fixture evidence and hard-eval movement without weakening hard evals, adding live source behavior, arbitrary local ingestion, or real binaries.

records.label: Article/scan fixture pack validation
records.writes: temporary files only where the archive eval runner builds a transient local index
records.failure_means: The Article/Scan Fixture Pack v0 report, article segment evidence, hard-eval satisfaction, or no-live/no-OCR/no-real-scan guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Added by Article/Scan Fixture Pack v0; validates tiny synthetic article-scan fixture evidence without adding OCR engines, PDF/image parsing, real scans, copyrighted article text, live source calls, or scraping.

records.label: Manual external baseline observation pack validation
records.writes: temporary files only where script tests create invalid observation examples
records.failure_means: The manual external baseline schema, systems, template, pending manifest, validator, or no-fabricated-baseline guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Added by Manual External Baseline Observation Pack v0; validates manual-only Google/Internet Archive observation protocol without scraping, automated querying, API calls, or observed baseline claims.

records.label: Manual Observation Batch 0 validation
records.failure_means: The Batch 0 manifest, pending slots, template, or no-fabricated-baseline guardrail regressed.
records.notes: Added by Manual Observation Batch 0; validates pending batch preparation only and performs no external searches.

records.label: Manual Observation Entry Helper validation
records.writes: temporary files only where helper tests create pending observation examples
records.failure_means: The manual observation entry helpers may list, create, validate, or report slots incorrectly, or may count pending/template files as observed.
records.notes: Added by Manual Observation Entry Helper v0; validates stdlib-only local entry tooling without scraping, URL fetching, browser automation, external queries, or fabricated observations.

records.label: Public static site validator
records.failure_means: The static public-site pack may have broken pages, missing source IDs, missing cautionary claims, prohibited claims, or unresolved local links.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_00 Static Public Site Pack; validates static documentation only and performs no deployment, network calls, live probes, or scraping.

records.label: Public static site pack tests
records.writes: temporary files only where validator tests copy the site pack
records.failure_means: The public static site pack or validator behavior regressed.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_00 Static Public Site Pack; checks no-JS static pages, source matrix honesty, prohibited claims, and validator JSON output without hosting or deployment behavior.

records.label: Public alpha rehearsal evidence check
records.failure_means: The public-alpha rehearsal evidence pack drifted from the static-site, smoke, route, eval, or external-baseline status it records.
records.notes: Added by Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0; validates local/static evidence only and performs no deployment, live source probing, scraping, or external observation.

records.label: Public alpha rehearsal evidence tests
records.failure_means: The rehearsal evidence pack, manifest, signoff status, blockers, or generator JSON/check behavior regressed.
records.notes: Added by Public Alpha Rehearsal Evidence v0; checks unsigned signoff and no-deployment/no-production/no-external-observation claims.

records.label: Public alpha wrapper config check
records.failure_means: The public-alpha wrapper no longer validates its safe default configuration.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_01 Production Public-Alpha Wrapper; validates config only and does not start a server, deploy, enable live probes, or add provider configuration.

records.label: Public alpha wrapper script tests
records.failure_means: The public-alpha wrapper CLI flags, JSON output, nonlocal-bind guard, live-probe guard, or path-redaction behavior regressed.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_01 Production Public-Alpha Wrapper; tests local helper behavior only, with no deployment, network calls, live probes, scraping, or external APIs.

records.label: Public alpha wrapper config/status tests
records.failure_means: The public-alpha wrapper config model or safe status projection regressed.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_01 Production Public-Alpha Wrapper; checks closed defaults and JSON-safe status fields without adding runtime deployment behavior.

records.label: Public alpha wrapper docs tests
records.failure_means: Wrapper docs or static-site references may have lost no-deployment, no-live-probe, or no-production caveats.
records.notes: Added by LIVE_ALPHA_01 Production Public-Alpha Wrapper; validates documentation claims only and does not deploy.

records.label: External baseline observation validator
records.failure_means: Committed manual baseline observation records are invalid or pending slot coverage drifted.
records.notes: Stdlib-only validator; it performs no external querying.

records.label: External baseline observation status report
records.failure_means: The manual baseline status report cannot summarize pending/observed coverage.
records.notes: Reports pending/observed manual baseline coverage without scraping, APIs, or automated searches.

records.label: External baseline observation list helper
records.failure_means: The manual baseline list helper cannot enumerate Batch 0 pending slots.
records.notes: Lists local pending/observed slots only; it performs no scraping, URL fetching, browser automation, or external queries.

records.label: External baseline observation create helper stdout
records.failure_means: The manual baseline create helper cannot emit a fillable pending observation record.
records.notes: Emits pending JSON only; it does not populate top results, fetch URLs, open browsers, query external systems, or mark records observed.

records.label: Hard Test Pack v0 aggregate
records.writes: temporary files only where called scripts create temp stores
records.failure_means: A high-risk eval truth, path-safety, drift, parity, source honesty, memory privacy, or repo-operating guard regressed.
records.notes: Added by Hard Test Pack v0; no product behavior, external scraping, or production-readiness claims.

records.label: Hard eval weakening guard
records.writes: temporary local index only through eval/audit scripts
records.failure_means: Hard archive-resolution or search-usefulness fixtures were weakened, removed, or stopped reporting current hard gaps honestly.
records.notes: Uses minimum thresholds and required IDs so adding harder queries remains allowed.

records.label: External baseline fabrication guard
records.failure_means: Google or Internet Archive baselines may have been marked observed without manual evidence, or docs/scripts imply scraping.
records.notes: Does not perform any external lookup.

records.label: Public-alpha path leakage guard
records.failure_means: Public-alpha status or blocked responses exposed private local path values or route inventory marked local-path routes safe.
records.notes: Uses sentinel paths and checks names, not values, are returned.

records.label: Route inventory drift guard
records.failure_means: The public-alpha route inventory drifted from known web/API routes or route-policy blocked constants.
records.notes: Uses bounded static checks and explicit exemptions.

records.label: README command drift guard
records.writes: temporary local index or fixture output only through safe commands
records.failure_means: README links or representative commands drifted from real repo paths/scripts.
records.notes: Avoids persistent server commands.

records.label: Documentation link drift guard
records.failure_means: A major local Markdown link no longer resolves.
records.notes: External URLs and generated anchors are intentionally out of scope.

records.label: Python oracle golden drift guard
records.failure_means: Python-oracle goldens drifted, required families disappeared, or private paths leaked into committed goldens.
records.notes: Delegates detailed parity fixture checks to the existing generator.

records.label: Rust parity structure guard
records.failure_means: Rust parity scaffolding drifted, was wired into Python runtime, or docs started implying active production Rust behavior.
records.notes: Does not require Cargo.

records.label: Source placeholder honesty guard
records.failure_means: Placeholder/future source records or public projections may be claiming implemented behavior.
records.notes: Keeps source inventory honesty distinct from future connector work.

records.label: Resolution memory privacy/path guard
records.failure_means: Resolution memory docs/goldens/audit outputs may leak private roots or imply cloud/shared/user-tracking behavior.
records.notes: A bounded privacy/path guard, not a complete privacy audit.

records.label: AIDE/test registry consistency guard
records.failure_means: AIDE queue/report scaffolding, test registry, or command matrix references drifted from one another.
records.notes: Does not require a local AIDE runtime.

records.label: Whitespace diff check
records.failure_means: Whitespace or conflict-marker issues are present in the working diff.
records.notes: Does not validate links; future docs drift guard should cover that.

records.label: Optional Rust workspace check
records.failure_means: The optional Rust skeleton or candidate seam does not compile in an environment with Cargo.
records.notes: Must not be required for normal Python verification while Cargo availability varies.

records.label: Optional Rust workspace tests
records.failure_means: Optional Rust parity tests failed in an environment with Cargo.
records.notes: Optional until the repo explicitly requires a Rust toolchain.
